response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering practices and nearly 20 years of professional experience, they fall critically short on mandatory requirements. They have only 6 months of professional C++ experience (from 2014-2015, approximately 10 years ago) versus the required 5-10 years, with recent career trajectory entirely in web/backend systems (Node.js, TypeScript, DLang). More critically, they lack any capital markets, fixed income, or financial services domain expertise—a core requirement for this role.
strengths: Extensive professional experience (19+ years) with demonstrated seniority and leadership capability
Strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, DevOps infrastructure, and proven communication abilities through workshops and open-source projects
Polyglot developer showing adaptability and ability to master new technical domains
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (only 6 months professional, ending ~10 years ago) against required 5-10 years; recent 10-year career has avoided C++ entirely
No Windows platform development experience; entire work history is Linux, Mac, web, or embedded systems
Complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services domain knowledge; career focused on web platforms, direct mail automation, and open-source

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (5-10 years required vs. 6 months actual)
Missing capital markets and financial services domain
